$comment: tdd: test-driven composition from specs - the assurance ladder (PLANS.md S1). read specs -> STUB (signatures only, panic bodies; oracle: compiles) -> TEST RED (author the test + a property/fuzz target from behavior; oracle tdd_red: compiles AND fails against the stub, so a trivial test is rejected) -> IMPL GREEN (fill bodies; oracle stage_files: go vet + go test -race pass) -> FUZZ (oracle go_fuzz: native FuzzXxx finds no crash) -> HARDEN (go_quality). Repairs are feedback cycles: red->test, green/fuzz/harden->impl, each re-generating with the oracle's output. Run: `ratchet flow . tdd --ws <proj>` after dropping specs in workspaces/<proj>/specs.
summary: Compose a Go module test-first: stub -> red test (+ property/fuzz) -> green impl -> fuzz -> harden, each gated and repaired by a real oracle.
